info.name: AEGIS Public API
info.description:
Postman v2.1 collection for the AEGIS Agent Identity & Verification API.

Set up the `AEGIS Local` environment (or your own) with `base_url`, `api_key`, `verify_key`, and the entity ids you want to exercise. Every authenticated request reads its credential from a Postman variable — no key material is ever literal in this file.

See `tools/postman/README.md` for the partner walk-through, and the `Denial Precedence Walk-through` folder for a reproducible tour through every `denialReason` AEGIS can emit.

exec: // 1. Generate a fresh idempotency key for every POST/PUT/PATCH so retries are safe.
// 2. Generate an X-Request-Id for traceability — the API echoes it in the audit log.
// - 4xx responses must carry a Round-15 `code` field for SDK switch-tables.
// - Verify denials (200 with valid:false) must surface `denialReason` from the canonical enum.
pm.test('2xx JSON has parseable body', function () {
pm.test('4xx body carries a `code` field', function () {

item.name: Health & Discovery
item.description: Public, unauthenticated surface. No API key required. Use these for liveness probes, version pinning, JWKS retrieval, and offline relying-party configuration.

request.description: Process liveness. Always 200 when the API process is up. No dependencies checked. Used by Railway / k8s for restart decisions.
Structured readiness. Returns 200 when DB+KMS are reachable (KMS is core because every audit row is signed). 503 when a core dep is down.
Returns `{ version, gitSha, builtAt }` for blue-green confirmation.
OIDC-style single-fetch discovery document. Every URL, the JWKS, the canonical denial-reason enum, the trust band ladder, supported runtimes, rate limits, and SDK package names.
JWKS view (RFC 8037) of the AEGIS audit-event signing key. Cacheable for one day. Lets a third party verify the chain offline.
Plain JSON helper — same key material as JWKS, easier to consume from non-JWK tooling.
Per-tier audit-event retention windows + redaction guarantees. Auto-derived from `apps/api/src/modules/billing/plans.ts` — never fabricated.

Per-tier pricing table (ADR-0014). Auto-derived from `apps/api/src/modules/billing/plans.ts`. JSON-shape note: `monthly_verify_quota: null` encodes the unbounded sentinel (FREE uses a lifetime cap; ENTERPRISE is uncapped). `monthly_price_cents: null` for ENTERPRISE (custom). `overage_per_call_e4` is in USD × 10⁻⁴.
Plain-text responsible-disclosure file. `Expires` field renewed at every deploy.
AI-agent-readable site description (Markdown). Lists every public surface an AI agent should hit.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Self-service API-key rotation with a 24h overlap window.

request.description: Rotate the calling API key. New plaintext returned EXACTLY ONCE — store it, then update `{{api_key}}`. The calling key remains valid for 24h, then is rejected with `expired_api_key`.

item.name: Billing
item.description: Stripe-backed commerce loop (M-011): checkout sessions, customer portal, plan summary. Webhook intake is signed by Stripe and excluded from this collection.

request.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session for a paid plan tier (DEVELOPER or GROWTH). Redirects via the returned `url` to Stripe-hosted Checkout. ENTERPRISE is sales-led; FREE has no purchase flow.

Returns a one-time Stripe Customer Portal URL where the customer can update card, cancel subscription, or download invoices. Requires a prior successful checkout (the principal must have a `stripeCustomerId`).
Plan tier + monthly usage snapshot for the calling principal. Reads Postgres + the Redis-backed verify counter — no Stripe round-trip.

item.name: Identity
item.description: Agent registration, lookup, listing, and revocation. All scoped to the calling principal (CLAUDE.md invariant 5).

request.description: Register a new agent identity. Provide your Ed25519 public key (base64url). AEGIS never holds your private key. The test script auto-stashes the returned `agentId` into `{{agent_id}}` for downstream requests.
Cursor-paginated list of agents owned by the calling principal, newest-first.
Get details for one agent owned by the calling principal.
Permanently revoke the agent. 204 on success. Cannot be undone.

item.name: Policy
item.description: Scoped permission policies. Every create returns an AEGIS-signed token the agent attaches to outbound requests.

request.description: Create a scoped permission policy. Stashes returned `policyId` and `signedToken` into `{{policy_id}}` / `{{policy_token}}`.
List active policies for the agent.
AEGIS does not currently expose a single-policy GET. Use the list endpoint and filter on `{{policy_id}}` client-side. Tracked as OpenAPI drift in tools/postman/README.md.
Instantly revoke a policy. Subsequent /verify calls referencing this policy return `valid:false` with `denialReason: POLICY_REVOKED`.

item.name: Verify
item.description: The relying-party hot path. Authenticated with the verify-only key (X-AEGIS-Verify-Key) — never the management key.

request.description: End-to-end verification: signature, policy scope, spend limit, BATE trust score. Target SLA <80ms p99. Note: 200 even when invalid — always inspect `valid` and `denialReason`.

item.name: Audit
item.description: Immutable, signed, append-only audit log (CLAUDE.md invariant 3). Per-agent paginated list and tenant-wide NDJSON stream for SOC2/FINRA evidence.

request.description: Paginated audit log for one agent. Use the returned `nextCursor` to walk forward.

item.name: Export audit events as NDJSON (per agent)

request.description: Stream the agent's audit log as newline-delimited JSON. Pipe through `jq` for ad-hoc analysis.
Tenant-wide NDJSON export. Memory-bounded via internal cursor pagination. The signature on each row + `/.well-known/audit-signing-key` lets a third party verify offline.

item.name: Webhooks
item.description: Subscribe an HTTPS callback URL to AEGIS events. The signing secret is shown once on creation — record it, then HMAC-SHA256 verify every inbound `X-Aegis-Signature` header.

request.description: Subscribe a callback URL. Returned `secret` (`whsec_…`) is shown ONCE — store it, then verify every delivery's `X-Aegis-Signature: t=<ts>,v1=<hmac>` header with HMAC-SHA256.
List webhook subscriptions for the calling principal.
Permanently delete the subscription. Idempotent — 204 even if it never existed.

item.name: BATE
item.description: Behavioral signal reporting (relying party → BATE) and trust-score retrieval (public).

request.description: Report a behavioral signal — feeds BATE. 202 (async) on accept. Phase-1 authorization: only the principal owning the agent may file reports against it.
Public endpoint — no API key required. Returns current `status`, `trustScore`, and `trustBand` for the agent. Used by relying parties for quick pre-checks before /verify.

item.name: Compliance
item.description: GDPR Art. 17 surface. Hash-committing redaction keeps the audit chain tamper-evident even after deletion (ADR-0006).

request.description: Redact one audit event by id. Per-principal isolation enforced inside the service. The chain still verifies because the signature commits to hashes, not raw values.

item.name: Redact every event for an agent

request.description: Bulk redact every audit event the calling principal owns for the named agent.

item.name: Onboarding
item.description: Activation-checklist surface. Drives the dashboard wizard and `aegis doctor` CLI.

request.description: Fetch the onboarding checklist for the caller's principal.
Mark one onboarding step complete. Idempotent — 204 on success.

item.name: Denial Precedence Walk-through
item.description:
Reproducible tour through every `denialReason` AEGIS can emit, in canonical precedence order (CLAUDE.md invariant 6 / ADR-0004 + ADR-0014 + ADR-0016).

Entries 1–10 hit POST /v1/verify with payloads contrived to trigger exactly that denial reason; each asserts `valid:false` and `denialReason` matches the expected string. Entry 11 (INTENT_MISMATCH) is emitted from POST /v1/intent/{manifestId}/actuals per ADR-0017 D3 (Phase 2 keeps intent denials in the dedicated /v1/intent/* response surface, not folded into /v1/verify); it asserts `recommendedDenialReason: 'INTENT_MISMATCH'` and a non-empty `mismatches[]` array.

Precedence (top wins):
  AGENT_NOT_FOUND → AGENT_REVOKED → INVALID_SIGNATURE → POLICY_REVOKED → POLICY_EXPIRED → SCOPE_NOT_GRANTED → TRIAL_EXHAUSTED → SPEND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED → TRUST_SCORE_TOO_LOW → ANOMALY_FLAGGED → INTENT_MISMATCH

request.description: Precondition: craft a token with a `sub` claim that matches no row in `AgentIdentity`. Easiest: take a working token and rewrite the agentId to a random ULID. Expect `denialReason: AGENT_NOT_FOUND` (precedence position 1).
Precondition: DELETE the agent (Identity → Revoke agent), then replay the previously-valid token within seconds. Expect `denialReason: AGENT_REVOKED` (position 2).
Precondition: take a working JWT and tamper with the signature segment (last `.`-separated chunk). Expect `denialReason: INVALID_SIGNATURE` (position 3).
Precondition: call DELETE /v1/agents/{agentId}/policies/{policyId} on the policy that minted `{{policy_token}}`, then replay the token. Expect `denialReason: POLICY_REVOKED` (position 4).
Precondition: create a policy with `expiresAt` 60s in the future, capture its `signedToken`, wait 60s, then verify. Expect `denialReason: POLICY_EXPIRED` (position 5).
Precondition: hold a policy that only grants `commerce` scope, then verify with `action: data-write.delete_account`. Expect `denialReason: SCOPE_NOT_GRANTED` (position 6).

Precondition: principal is on the FREE_TRIAL plan and has consumed >= `trialVerifiesCap` (default 10,000 per ADR-0014). Any subsequent verify denies with `denialReason: TRIAL_EXHAUSTED` (position 7) — distinct from `PLAN_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` (paid-tier monthly cap) and `SPEND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` (per-policy spend cap). Maps to HTTP 200 verify response with `valid:false`. The pre-algorithm billing path may also surface this as HTTP 402 outside the verify envelope.
Precondition: create a policy with `maxPerTransaction: 500`, then verify with `amount: 999999`. Expect `denialReason: SPEND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` (position 8).

Precondition: hammer the agent with `eventType: fraud_confirmed, severity: critical` reports until BATE pushes its trust score below the relying party's minimum band (FLAGGED). Expect `denialReason: TRUST_SCORE_TOO_LOW` (position 9).

Precondition: send a context block with strong anomaly signals (high velocity, impossible geo jump, brand-new merchant for a long-running agent). The BATE realtime layer flags it. Expect `denialReason: ANOMALY_FLAGGED` (position 10).

Precondition: issue an intent manifest first via POST /v1/intent declaring a bounded action (e.g. action='acp.payment', merchantId='ACME-FLORIST', amountCap=$200, strictness='strict'). Capture the returned `manifestId` into the environment variable {{manifest_id}}. Then POST actuals that VIOLATE the declared intent (this fixture sends a wrong merchantId AND an amount over the cap to trigger two mismatches). Under strict reconciliation, expect HTTP 200 with `recommendedDenialReason: 'INTENT_MISMATCH'` and a non-empty `mismatches[]` array containing entries with `kind: 'wrong-merchant'` and `kind: 'over-amount-cap'`.

Wire-surface note: INTENT_MISMATCH is emitted from POST /v1/intent/{manifestId}/actuals, NOT POST /v1/verify (per ADR-0017 D3 — Phase 2 keeps intent denials in the dedicated /v1/intent/* response surface). The other 10 walk-through entries above all hit /v1/verify; this one hits the intent reconciliation endpoint. Precedence position 11 (per `DENIAL_REASON_PRECEDENCE` in `packages/types/src/constants.ts`, ADR-0016 DECISION 3).

Cross-RP travel: the same reconciliation also fires `INTENT_MISMATCH_OBSERVED` to BATE (`apps/api/src/modules/bate/bate.weights.ts:57` — `-100` per signal, `300` per-window cap). The agent's trust score drops; subsequent /v1/verify calls for the same agent against ANY relying party may return `TRUST_SCORE_TOO_LOW` (position 9) once the band cutoff is crossed.
